game.title: Minigame Madness
game.short_text: Frenetic Minigames

details.title: Minigame Madness
details.description:
Try to master all minigames in different speeds and difficulties!
Beat 20 Minigames to win the game (and unlock the Challenge Mode)
How to play:
Use Arrow keys (or WASD) or Spacebar to play the Minigames. Each one has its own controls shown in the bottom right of the screen when playing it.
Features:
2 frenetic gamemodes8+ different minigames3 speed levels3 difficulty levels
Assets used:
Art - Kenney's Bit Pack
Music and SFX - OpenGameArt

If you are having trouble running the game in your browser, try another browser (Chrome can sometimes block some resources) or download the game.

resultData.title: May I Have Your Attention, Please - Jam Version

game.title: May I Have Your Attention, Please - Jam Version
game.short_text: Play four minigames simultaneously!  How long can you last?

details.title: May I Have Your Attention, Please - Jam Version
details.description:
Play four minigames simultaneously!  Each round is shorter than the last - how long can you survive?
Games on the left side of the screen use WASD and mouse.Games on the right side of the screen use the arrow keys and mouse.
Made in 48 hours over 9 days for the Miz Game Jam:https://itch.io/jam/miz-jam-1
My notes:
When I first saw the art kit, I was blown away by how many different sprites there were, and for so many different genres.  I immediately wondered how I could use as many different sprites as possible, and so I had the idea to try making minigames from all different genres.
I tried to repurpose and creatively use sprites when I could.  As an example, in the space shooter game, I used a house sprite to create the ship's cockpit and swords to build the ship's frame.  I also used a tree sprite to make asteroids.
I hope you guys have as much fun playing this as I did making it!
Credits:
Werq by Kevin MacLeodLink: https://incompetech.filmmusic.io/song/4616-werqLicense: http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/
Tech Live by Kevin MacLeodLink: https://incompetech.filmmusic.io/song/4463-tech-liveLicense: http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/
Ouroboros by Kevin MacLeodLink: https://incompetech.filmmusic.io/song/4996-ouroborosLicense: http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/
Sound effects from www.freesound.org and from me.
"Lychee Soda" font from www.fontenddev.com.

game.title: Treasure Cave
game.short_text: Puzzle & Adventure game created for MizJam 1

details.title: Treasure Cave
details.description:
Life in the village is soooo boring... let's go. Join me on this adventure and I promise we'll find some awesome treasures on the way!

How To Play
Play with WASD or arrow keys. Once you move you have to wait until you hit a wall to move again.

Try to get as much coins and diamonds as you can! Post your record on the comments!Complete the 10 levels to have a big reward :)If you get stuck on a level due to a bug, use the Restart Level button on the top right of the screen

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
Comments from Developer:
This was my first Game Jam experience, this game was done in under 48 hours of development. This was also my first experience creating a game with Godot, I just started learning it 3 months ago. I do have game development experience creating Flash games but last one I created was more than 6 years ago. I've been mainly focused on other types of software development (mobile apps and web). Since I graduated as a software engineer last year I have more time now and very recently I decided to come back to the Game Development world as a hobbie, and here I am :) 
During the week this game jam lasted I worked full-time on my day job and I worked hard on this game at night so it was a VERY exhausting week for me. I didn't do anything else other than work the hole week really to get done as much as I could for this game. But I think it was worth it. All in all I'm very proud of the results :)
During the development process I kept a Dev Log on a Google Docs that you can access here (but please play the game first since it contains spoilers!): Google Docs
Special Thanks to my girlfriend Vero that supported me the hole week, and to my brother Guille that created the music and sound effects for the game.
If you loved the music and sounds consider hiring him for your next project ;) Guillermo Dorfman - guillermodorfman@gmail.com
I hope you enjoy the game as much as I enjoyed creating it. If you do please leave a comment bellow :)

game.title: Townfolks
game.short_text: A short townbuilding RPG (1h-2h)

details.title: Townfolks
details.description:
WHAT IS TOWNFOLKS ?
Help a doomed island fight back against an evil wizard in this town-building RPG.
Battle monsters, get gold, rebuild your town and upgrade your gear. But be careful, you don't have much time left...
Controls : Clic on stuff to do stuff
NB : this is a 30min - 1h game
This game saves every time a day end, so feel free to stop and come back later, I'll be waiting for you ! 
Don't feel like playing on your browser ? Download the game as an executable !
You'll find the original Unity 2019.4 jam project (1.3) as a .zip bellow, download it and mess around, maybe you'll find some cool unity / c# tips hidden inside.
DEVLOG

Sorry if I missed some details in the devlog, didn't felt like posting a 30 min video. If you want to know more, leave a comment here and I'll try to respond !
"Only took an hour and a half ! That went by pretty quick though" - Miziziziz
UPDATES
1.4 : Adventure bounty feature + Achievements + New ennemies + Fixed a lot of bugs1.3 : Fixed some late game balancing issues + improved the hunter power descriptions + fixed the post processing that wasn't affecting some UI elements1.2 : Fixed some balancing issues (rapid fire too powerful & moved day limit from 50 days to 40 days) 1.1 : Fixed some sprites in the bad ending animation, and game breaking bug when restarting a new game after completing one
SUPPORT ME

details.title: Hungover Hero
details.description:
Metroid Prime-style game about finding something to get over your hangover.Find and collect 2 weapons and 4 upgrades, before facing your greatest challenge...
AFTER SUBMITTING I NOTICED THE WEB VERSION DIDN'T CAPTURE THE MOUSE. I HAD TO RE-EXPORT AS WINDOWS AND UPLOAD THAT. MY APOLOGIES!
EDIT: I have now uploaded a version that should capture the mouse when clicked! No other changes were made!
Controls:WASD - MoveSpace - JumpClick - AttackQ/Mouse Wheel - Switch Weapons (When they are found)
Made with: Godot, Audacity, Paint.net, Trenchbroom, Qodot, Freesound.org, Incompetech.filmmusic.io
Art is from Kenney.nl, https://kenney.nl/assets/bit-pack
I unfortunately ran out of time, and the final cutscene piece of the game has no voiceover. This causes the text to scroll by before you can read it, unfortunately. You also cannot exit on the final screen besides Alt+f4 on the Windows version. I plan to add it all in post-jam (in a marked POST-JAM version).
There's a bug with the html version, which sometimes makes shadows not work properly. If that bothers you, please try the windows version.
I've uploaded a windows version that's gles3 that fixes the problem, I believe.
Attribution and resources used: https://pastebin.com/aebXSq5x
Current windows download is the post-jam version, it includes a voice line I missed, and makes the boss more fair.
Dev log will be coming in the next few days eventually!Download and Unzip the archive, and run HungoverHero.exe, make sure to keep HungoverHero.pck in the same directory

game.title: Raiders of Mizburgh
game.short_text: MizJam 1 entry by Peter Offerman

details.title: Raiders of Mizburgh
details.description:
RAIDERS OF MIZBURGHYou are a knight of Mizburgh. All your knight buddies have left to plunder other towns, and they've left you to defend the city. This game was completed within 24 hours of work, spread across 6 days. It's an entry for MizJam1: a gamejam where each game uses the same art assets. These assets were provided by the amazing Kenney. If you're into game development but don't know him, do yourself a favor and check out his website.GOALProtect the treasures of Mizburgh from the looters. Enemies will try to take the amazing treasures, like the diamond, the crown, and the cyclops' eye. I don't know why they kept the eye, but whatever. Stay alive, and keep at least 1 treasure in the city. Endure 10 waves of enemies, upgrade your skills between waves, and emerge victorious.
CONTROLS
ButtonActionWASD/Arrow keysMove aroundAimMouseSelect sword/shield1Select bow2AttackLeft mouse buttonBlock (shield)Right mouse button
SOURCEIf you want to check out how the game works, take a look at the source code at https://gitlab.com/peterofferman/mizjam1_peterofferman.Many of the implemented solutions are hacky, quick ways to get stuff done. It's a gamejam game after all. If you have any questions about the game and its development, let me know.ABOUT ME
My name is Peter Offerman, and I make video games for fun. Because I'm unable to support myself and my family with this amazing hobby, I make a living by programming business software.peterofferman.comTwitter.com/GamesByPeter

game.title: Stellar King
game.short_text: Overscoped Medieval-Space-Strategy-Sim in weird retro style!

details.title: Stellar King
details.description:
Controls:
W,A,S,D and Mouse
To progress a year CLICK on the Throne.
Try to get as much Prestige and Population as possible before the king dies.

Please be aware that I added the html version later on because it's more convenient to play. It's not optimized for it though, you may have frame drops and music glitches. So I suggest the desktop version instead.
Made for Miz Jam 1This was a 2 person project.

Stellar King is a strategy/simulation Space game, but you are a medieval king. Manage your servants and alien emissaries. Colonize planets, let your population grow to the highest numbers, gain prestige to be remembered for ever!
Originally, the idea was to make a fantasy game where you have to manage your court as the king. My wife wanted to go more into the space direction, so we did both! I was listening to 80s synth music which then led to the final aesthetic style.
I had way to little time for actual playtesting. I wanted to include more sound effects, the balance is horrible and many calculations probably not how I imagined them. And be sure there will be bugs. I had more NPCs planned and they all should have been more unique. Unfortunately, the Queen is pretty useles right now. And allthought it should have been a major mechanic, there aren't any negative consequences for letting relations go low. I couldn't do that in time, at the end I prioritized a Start Menu / End Screen and the tutorial. Obviously all done in a hurry, but I hope the game is playable right now, let me know! :)
Besides that, I'm really happy with the game. It's only my second Game Jam after one in late 2018. And it's amazing to see how much I have improved. That gives me hope for the future!

A huge thanks to Miziziziz for hosting this Jam and especially for the way it's organized. I can completly participate even with a full time job. That's amazing!

game.title: Deal With The Devil
game.short_text: The Devil cheated you out of your soul. Now it's your turn to kick some ***.

details.title: Deal With The Devil
details.description:
*I forgot to mention that you can exit the game to the main menu at any time by pressing the Escape Key!**Also you can jump through floating platforms, going through a platform resets your double jump!*
Greetings, everyone! This right here is my submission to the Mizi Game Jam 2020 Event! In this Game Jam we only had 48 Hours to complete a game using a predetermined art pack!Deal With The Devil is a short and typical platformer in which you take the reins of a nameless soul, trying to convince the Devil to give him eternal life. Of course the Devil had a slight trick up his sleeve, and after signing the eternal contract, you are immediately sent directly to Hell. It's up to you to track down that pesky Devil and redeem your soul!I apologize for any bugs xD I playtested as much as I could, but you know, we humans make mistakes. Please feel free to share your feedback in the comments below. I will have a DevLog discussing my process of tackling this project! You can check out my YouTube channel  HERE!

game.title: Squeak Greek Antique
game.short_text: antique tycoon / simulation game made for miz jam 1

details.title: Squeak Greek Antique
details.description:
Squeak Greek Antique tells a story of a man, a Greek immigrant in New York, who's living a hard life in the dim reality of the 21st century. His father is no longer alive and that leaves him in charge of the family business - the antique shop. With the help of father's former friends, he puts his father's shop back on its feet and eventually moves back to his homeland.

Controls (supports controller):
WASD, arrow keys (D-pad) - movement
spacebar (cross, A) - use, confirm
ESC (circle, B) - cancel

Attributes
smart, dicker, bargain - improves your chances to make a better deal
greek - learn greek language to level 9 (and have 3000$) to achieve end game goal
You can increase attributes at you laptop or earn some extra bucks in there (use fiverr). 

This game is part of Miz Jam 1. It must have been done uder 48 hours of work. Theme was common art kit:  https://kenney.nl/assets/bit-pack

Game is missing some planned features like load/save, more items variety, game menu, better code. But the time is limited. I still consider myself a beginner (I finished my 1st game 3 months ago)
Enjoy!
24.08.2020 21:37 reuploaded since some gamefiles were missing. Download, unpack and play.

game.title: Skeletonlike
game.short_text: Skeletonlike ​is a local co-op side-scrolling beat-em-up made for Miz Jam 1

details.title: Skeletonlike
details.description:
Skeletonlike is a side-scrolling beat-em-up made for Miz Jam 1 (08/23/2020), programmed by JordanXion (Itch: Jordan4762) and art/design by Nuggetzrago (Itch: Trex373)
Skeletonlike supports 1-4 players (Local Co-op). 1 person can play on keyboard, with 3 people on controllers, or alternatively all 4 players can use controllers.  Tested with Xbox One and PS4 controllers on Windows.
Please leave a rating and any Comments/Suggestions/Bug Reports etc in the comment section below, it would be hugely appreciated! 
Controls:Join Game: Enter / Controller Start (Per keyboard / controller player)
Restart Level: J / Controller Select
Move: WASD / Left Joystick / D-Pad
Light Attack: Left Arrow / Xbox X / PS4 Square
Heavy Attack: Right Arrow / Xbox B / PS4 Circle
Jump: Up Arrow / Xbox Y / PS4 Triangle

Known Issues/Bugs:
In multiplayer, players can walk off cameraMoving camera appears jitteryLeaf shadows in Forest section appear checkered on WebGLIf player joins game after initial players have already walked away from spawn, they will be far off screen

game.title: Monster Casino
game.short_text: The Gambling RPG where you can coerce monsters to cheat for you.

details.title: Monster Casino
details.description:
Chelly and Dog, citizens of Hell, are forced into a gauntlet of high stakes Poker death matches in order to secure their rent, which was unfairly jacked up by Satan.
With her high emotional intelligence, Chelly is able to recruit monsters to her side. Using the secret technique of "cheating", these monsters can tip the scales in her favor.
Created in 48 hours for Miz Jam 1.
Source code available here: https://github.com/billymonks/monster-casino
Instructions
Monster Casino is played exclusively using the mouse and left mouse button. Cutscenes can be skipped with the space bar.
When exploring, click on the ground to walk to a location, and click on a monster to interact with them. Some monsters will challenge you to a poker battle when interacted with.
Poker battles play out similarly to standard Texas Hold 'em, with an added twist: monsters in your party can cheat to assist you during play. To activate these cheats on your turn, just click on the party member's icon on the bottom-left corner of the screen. When you hover the mouse over the icon a tooltip will be displayed showing your party member's ability, and how much "security alert %" the ability will cost to use. The current "security alert %" can be seen on your turn on the top-right corner of the screen, designated by a yellow "!". If an ability would bring this alert level above 100%, you will be unable to use the ability.
Texas Hold 'em basics
Aside from that, battles play out like standard Texas Hold 'em. The objective of the game is to bankrupt your opponent by betting on who has the stronger hand.
Each player receives two cards (known as "hole cards", which are hidden from the other player. Five additional cards are placed on the table, known as "community cards". A player's hand value is calculated from the strongest possible 5 card hand from that player's hole cards and the community cards.
Game flow
Each turn, player and opponent take turns betting as cards are revealed.
First, before any cards have been dealt, one player pays the "small blind", and the other pays the "big blind". This money goes into the "pot". The pot is a pile of money on the table that is at stake for the current hand.
Next, each player is dealt their two hole cards and are given the opportunity to bet.
Next turn, 3 of the 5 community cards are revealed. Both player have an opportunity to bet.
Next, an additional community card is revealed and both players have another opportunity to bet.
Next, the last community card is revealed and both players get one last chance to bet.
Finally, if neither player has folded, it's time for the showdown. Both players' hole cards are revealed, and the player with the strongest hand wins the pot.
After each hand, the price of the big blind and small blind doubles, and which player is paying the big blind or small blind, and who plays first each turn (known as the dealer) switches.
Betting
Each turn, each player has the opportunity to increase the stakes, match the stakes, or fold and give up the pot. The player's options are: "Check" (if both players are currently betting the same stakes, checking passes your turn without betting any additional money.), "Call" (place money into the pot to match the opponent's stake and stay in the current hand), "Fold" (surrender the pot to the other player, ending the current hand and not placing any more money into the pot), or "Raise" (to match the opponent's stake, and bet additional money in the pot. If the opponent would like to stay in the current hand, they will have to "Call" your additional bet, or else surrender the pot.)
Hand strength from strongest to weakest
NameDescriptionExampleRoyal FlushA straight flush from 10 to AceStraight FlushA straight of the same suitFour of a kindFour cards of the same valueFull houseThree of a kind and a pairFlushFive cards of the same suitStraightFive cards in sequential orderThree of a kindThree cards of the same valueTwo pairsTwo sets of pairsPairTwo cards with the same value
In the case that the player and opponent have the same hand, the hand with the higher value will win. (Example: a pair of Kings will beat a pair of Queens. If both players have the same pair, the tie will be split by who has the highest value card in their hand outside that pair.)
In the event that both players have an identically valued hand, the pot will be split among both.
Credits
Kenney - 1-Bit Pack 1-Bit PackKenney - 54 Casino sound effects 54 Casino sound effectsSHADOWFOX2 - Casino, title, credits music SHADOWFOX2Maoudamashii - Battle themes and many sfx Maoudamashiibilly - Designer and developer dairy.dog
Interested in the future of Monster Casino's development? Join the discord!

game.title: Chainitaire
game.short_text: Solitaire game about making long chains of cards.

details.title: Chainitaire
details.description:
Chainitaire is a solitare type score attack game made for #mizjam1 where the gist was that everyone was using the same art kit.
You play by placing cards on the playing field making big chains of cards connected by same suit or card value and get more points the more successful links you make with a single placement. There are also some bonus items on the field which give multipliers to score gained from the card on the same cell.
Do you have what it takes to climb to the top of the leaderboards?
Tools used:
Unity 2019.3BeepBox.coGarageBandBfxr
Source code:https://github.com/anttihaavikko/chainitaire

game.title: The Technomagical Method
game.short_text: A tough, silly puzzle game about gnomes, inductive logic and BIG BRAINS.

details.title: The Technomagical Method
details.description:
IMPORTANT:
If you experience lag or stuttering, download the version for your system, below.Press 1 for hints (you get told about them at level 4)Press 9 to instantly win a level if you get stuck, to see all the writing
All of these "alchemists" and "sorcerors" running around with their haphazard, chaotic "experiments".
You are not like them. You are a scientist!
So dust off your alchemical tablet, gather your lizard skulls, your fire salts, your lab gnomes and your cheese and crack the secrets of alchemy once and for all!

The Technomagical Method is a tough puzzle game about the scientific method. Every ritual has secret rules that you must gradually crack with inductive logic: "Why does this work and not that? What do these patterns have in common".
With a tutorial, tips, and greedy gnome helpers, you might, MIGHT be able to untangle the rules of magic.
Devlog video:
